commit.message:
fix(model-3d): merge coplanar wall faces to prevent stretch artifacts

After Case 3 extrude, gap-filling triangle walls share vertices with
moved corners. Subsequent extrudes move those corners, stretching the
triangles into large sloping faces. Fix: merge coplanar wall faces
into adjacent side faces immediately after creation. The side face
absorbs the triangle into a larger polygon that stretches correctly.

commit.message:
Add listening progress ring to guide users to play long enough

Shows a circular progress indicator that fills as the user plays.
Tolerates brief pauses (up to 3s for rests/breathing) without
resetting. Drains on extended silence. Collapses with a smooth
transition once the first tune is identified. Keeps users engaged
through the ~6s needed for reliable identification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: cloud span parser — accept alternate JSON keys, strip markdown fences

OpenAI returns {"extractions": [...]} or {"data": [...]} instead of
{"fields": [...]}. Anthropic wraps JSON in ```json ``` fences. Both
caused 0 spans to be parsed despite valid extraction data.

Also handles single-span flat dict responses (wrap in list).

commit.message:
Add OTLP JSON support to HTTP receiver

Content-Type negotiation per OTLP spec:
- application/json → protojson.Unmarshal
- application/x-protobuf (default) → proto.Unmarshal

Applies to both /v1/traces and /v1/metrics endpoints.
Responses match the request format.

Unblocks shell scripts and curl-based tooling from sending
spans and metrics without protobuf encoding.

commit.message:
Fix double-suffix bug in result_key: _processed_processed.png

The chained .replace() calls caused the .png from the first replace to be
matched by the subsequent .replace(".png", "_processed.png"), producing
an invalid key that failed R2 download validation. Now strips the extension
first, then appends _processed.png.

EMI-150: Fix stale session recovery requeuing fix-phase tasks as ready (#157)

When a fix-phase session hit a stale session error, the recovery handler
requeued the task as `ready` instead of `changes_requested`, causing the
next dispatch to start a fresh implementation on a new branch and abandon
the existing PR and review feedback.

Fix: check `reviewCycleCount > 0` to distinguish fix-phase tasks from
fresh implementations, and requeue accordingly.

Initial commit: multi-supplier order routing API

.NET 8 ASP.NET Core REST API that routes multi-item DME orders to optimal
suppliers based on product capability, geographic coverage, customer
satisfaction, and shipment consolidation.

commit.message:
feat: git worktree support with per-worktree DBs (#25)

* feat: git worktree support with per-worktree DBs

Detect linked worktrees via filesystem inspection of .git file (no
subprocess calls). Add {worktree} placeholder for db_path config so
each worktree gets its own index. Seed new worktree DBs from main's
index using SQLite backup API to avoid cold rebuilds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review feedback for worktree support

- Use Git's stable worktree ID from .git/worktrees/<id> instead of
  checkout directory basename for worktree_name, preventing collisions
- Change is_linked() to check main_root.is_some() instead of name != "main"
- Resolve seed DB path against main worktree root, not linked worktree root
- Use temp file + atomic rename in seed_db to avoid leaving partial DBs on failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: seed_db_path now handles relative/default db_path correctly

Compare resolved paths instead of checking for {worktree} in the raw
config string. This ensures linked worktrees with relative or default
db_path values (e.g. .shire/index.db) correctly seed from the main
worktree's DB rather than forcing a cold rebuild.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add regression test for explicit relative db_path seeding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use reserved _primary worktree name to prevent ID collision

The primary worktree's worktree_name was "main", which could collide
with a linked worktree whose Git ID is also "main" (under
.git/worktrees/main), causing both to resolve to the same DB path.

Use the reserved constant "_primary" for the primary worktree to
guarantee unique per-worktree DB paths. Includes a regression test
for the collision scenario.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Add MCP server support for dynamic external tool integration

MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets shellm connect to external tool servers
(filesystem, databases, APIs) without code changes — just add to mcp_servers.json.
Includes async bridge with dedicated daemon thread, lazy connection, graceful
degradation, and namespaced tool routing (server__tool).

commit.message:
Fix: KeyError 'implementation' on agent handoff routing

Root cause: when consultant hands off to implementation, state carries
handoff_requested=True/handoff_target='implementation'. The implementation
node's permission gate and step-limit early returns didn't reset those fields.
On next routing, agent_should_continue saw stale handoff state, returned
'implementation', which is not in the implementation node's own edge map.

Fixes:
- All early returns in all three agent nodes now explicitly reset
  handoff_requested/target/reason to prevent stale state
- agent_should_continue guards against self-handoff (returns 'end')
  as a safety net for any future stale state scenarios

commit.message:
fix: OCR picks wrong table - metadata labels treated as line items

Root cause: The bill has a 7-row metadata table (Invoice No., Delivery Note,
Reference No., Buyer's Order No., etc.) on the right side, plus a 1-row
product table (AIFACE MARS-WIFI). The old code picked the LARGEST table
(7 rows wins over 1 row), so metadata labels became line items.

Fixes:
1. pickBestItemTable(): score tables by SUM OF AMOUNTS, not row count.
   Real product tables have amounts like 12508.72; metadata tables have 0.
2. METADATA_LABEL_RE: regex filter for known Indian bill field labels
   (Invoice No., Delivery Note, Reference No., Buyer's Order No., etc.)
3. Skip rows with no numeric data at all (pure label rows)
4. Better qty/rate extraction: try all (qi, ri) pairs where qi×ri ≈ amount
   within 25% tolerance — correctly handles Disc.% column in between
   (e.g. [1, 12764, 2%, 12508.72] → qty=1, rate=12764)

fix: clear agent_ensured on stage transitions to fix re-entry notification (#257)

When an issue bounced back to a custom agent stage (e.g., independent_review
→ ci_wait → debug → independent_review), agent_ensured was never cleared,
so the heartbeat thought the reviewer was already notified and skipped

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

AIR Compute Model Explanation (#1383)

* Add AIR compute model documentation

Documents the architecture-independent AIR programming model: the
three-level hierarchy (launch/segment/herd), the L3/L2/L1 memory
spaces, and the semantics of each dialect operation. Includes
backend-specific sections explaining how the model maps onto AMD NPU
(AIE) hardware and AMD GPU (ROCDL/HIP), with a side-by-side summary
table. Links the new doc from the README.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: refine air.launch, air.segment and air.herd semantics

- Add sync/async syntax variants for all three operations
- Document may-be-parallel semantics for air.launch and prohibit
  the concurrency attribute; add allowed affinity and depend lists
- Distinguish always-parallel (air.herd) from may-be-parallel (air.launch)
- Document temporal and spatial constraints expressible in segment/herd bodies
- Add compile-time worst-case resource analysis requirement for air.segment:
  L2 memory, compute tile count, and DMA channel bounds must be statically
  determined and used for fit verification, resource assignment, and
  co-residency guarantees

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify air.segment iteration space and resource scaling

All iterations of a segment's optional iteration space must be mappable
in parallel; the runtime guarantees overlapping instance lifetimes. The
compile-time resource analysis is split into two stages: per-instance
worst-case analysis followed by multiplication by the total iteration
count to obtain the aggregate hardware requirement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: expand compute model with token taxonomy and MI3xx four-level hierarchy

- Add §1.5 Token taxonomy and operation lifetimes: defines resource vs
  execution lifespans, token alloc/scope rules, and the three token kinds
  (affinity, concurrency, dependency) with their effects on both lifespan
  dimensions.

- Update §2.3 air.herd: iteration space defines a contiguous resource block;
  add platform-specific semantics subsection covering AIE reshape attribute
  and GPU MI3xx warp/CU mapping; correct synchrony table (default is
  synchronous, async keyword makes it asynchronous).

- Rewrite §4.2 MI3xx device-scale mapping as a four-level hierarchy:
  unit-iteration-space launch → outer segment (cardinality 8, XCD granularity)
  → inner segment (CUs per XCD) → herd (≤32 warps). Document LDS (L2) and
  global (L3) as the intra-herd communication memory spaces.

- Add backend-granularity attribute table to §2.2 air.segment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: introduce !air.token with dependency/affinity/concurrency attribute lists

Replace the untyped air.async.token / async keyword mechanism with a unified
!air.token type consumed via named attribute lists on all hierarchy ops:

- air.token.alloc creates a free-standing token for grouping ops under a
  shared affinity or concurrency constraint.
- Op results remain the second creation mechanism; omitting the result gives
  the synchronous (blocking) form of the op.
- dependency = [...]: happens-before ordering; compiler may reuse resources
  across the edge (direct successor to air.async.token chains).
- affinity   = [...]: binds ops to the same hardware partition; implies
  disjoint resource and execution lifespans.
- concurrency = [...]: requires overlapping execution lifetimes; implies
  simultaneously live resource sets (co-residency enforcement).
- air.launch restricts token crossing: only dependency tokens may enter a
  launch body; affinity/concurrency tokens may not (would contradict
  may-be-parallel semantics).

Update §1.4, §1.5, and syntax blocks for air.launch, air.segment, air.herd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: resolve air.launch synchrony ambiguity; purge air.async.token references

Gap 2: the old 'Default (async)' synchrony tables contradicted the token model
introduced in gap 1, and air.async.token remained in several prose sections.

Changes:
- Define synchrony uniformly across all three hierarchy ops: presence of a
  result value [%token =] makes an op asynchronous; absence of a result (or
  the explicit 'sync' keyword) makes it synchronous. The sync keyword is now
  described as a readable synonym for the no-result form, not a separate mode.
- Rewrite Synchrony tables for air.launch and air.segment with three explicit
  rows (async result / implicit sync / explicit sync) to remove the ambiguous
  'Default (async)' label.
- Replace all remaining air.async.token references outside §1.5 with !air.token:
  air.segment semantics prose, §2.6 air.execute / air.wait_all, §5 summary table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: extend segment resource analysis to recurse through nested segments

Gap 3: the Stage 1/Stage 2 analysis only handled air.herd inside a flat
segment; nested air.segment bodies (required for the MI3xx four-level model)
were not covered and the nesting constraint explicitly disallowed them.

Changes:
- Mark the analysis as recursive and bottom-up: inner segments are fully
  analysed before the segments that contain them.
- Split Stage 1 into two cases:
  - Leaf segment (body has air.herd, no nested air.segment): unchanged.
  - Containing segment (body has nested air.segment ops): each nested segment
    is reduced to an opaque (tiles, L2, DMA) footprint via its own Stage 1+2,
    then the outer Stage 1 computes the max over concurrently active footprints.
    Concurrency is determined from the outer async dependency graph: dependency-
    only ordering permits resource reuse; no ordering or concurrency tokens
    require simultaneous allocation.
- Clarify Stage 2 applies independently at each nesting level; aggregate
  resources multiply by the product of all iteration counts along the path.
- Update nesting constraints to explicitly permit air.segment inside
  air.segment; state that air.herd is the innermost level (may not contain
  air.segment or air.herd); note depth is unbounded in the model with
  backends imposing device-specific limits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: specify channel capacity, flow control, balance, and deadlock conditions

Gap 4: air.channel had no statement on buffer depth, blocking semantics,
whether put/get pairs must be statically balanced, or when deadlock arises.

Changes to §2.5:

Syntax — add depth attribute to channel declaration; show async forms of
put/get with !air.token result and dependency list, consistent with gap 1.

Capacity and depth — depth attribute (default 1) controls how many transfers
may be in-flight simultaneously. Depth 1 = rendezvous; depth 2 = double-
buffering; depth N = N outstanding transfers before producer stalls.

Flow control semantics — put blocks when channel is at capacity; get blocks
when channel is empty. Async form dispatches immediately but the returned
!air.token does not signal until the blocking condition resolves; the
flow-control constraint is not removed by using the async form.

Balance requirement — static balance condition: along every execution path
the number of puts and gets at each channel index must be equal. Covers
loop bodies (per-iteration balance), mismatched herd iteration spaces
(total transfer count must balance), and conditional branches (per-branch
balance). Violation is a compile-time error.

Deadlock conditions — a program deadlocks when put/get are mutually waiting
with no concurrent context to break the wait. Documents the minimal deadlock
case (put and get in the same sequential scope, depth 1). States the
necessary condition for deadlock freedom: every channel must have a
concurrent execution context in which its matching put and get can both
proceed, and the communication graph must be acyclic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: specify scf.for iter_arg pipelining and scf.parallel init/reduce semantics

Gap 5 (revised): initial draft was based on a heuristic 'token escape' model.
After reading the actual AIRDependency pass and test cases (tests 46-51), the
correct mechanism is explicit iter_args and init/reduce, not implicit escape.

scf.for:
- Iterations are logically sequential but iter_args enable controlled overlap.
  The token yielded by scf.yield at end of iteration N becomes the iter_arg
  of N+1; ops that depend on it wait, ops that don't may run concurrently.
- Pipeline depth D = number of independent iter_arg token chains maintained
  for a given resource type. D=1: strictly sequential. D=2: double-buffering
  (compute from N overlaps prefetch for N+1). D>2: multiple independent
  in-flight streams.
- L2: loop-carried allocs (live across scf.yield) are permanently live; per-
  iteration allocs reuse one slot. Compute tiles and DMA channels multiply by D.
- Add a concrete scf.for IR example showing the iter_arg pattern.

scf.parallel:
- All instances start concurrently once the init token signals (broadcast dep).
- scf.reduce block performs pairwise tree-reduction of instance tokens using
  air.wait_all; result signals only when all instances complete.
- Resource bounds: all instances concurrent, so sum (not max) per-instance
  footprints, exactly as for a segment iteration space.
- Add a concrete scf.parallel IR example showing init/reduce pattern.

Update Stage 1 intro to list four structural cases (was two, then three).
Update segment Semantics prose to mention scf.parallel alongside scf.for.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify may-be-parallel launch semantics, pipelining patterns, backend asymmetry

Gap 6: air.launch may-be-parallel semantics were stated but not explained —
the document never said why the constraint exists, what the launch iteration
space is actually for, or how to express pipelining between output tiles.

Add three subsections to §2.1 air.launch Semantics:

When to use the launch iteration space:
- Expresses a pool of independent work units for runtime scheduling.
- Does NOT guarantee simultaneous execution; use air.segment iteration space
  (always-concurrent, co-residency guaranteed) when that is required.
- Appropriate when: instances are self-contained, tile count is runtime-
  determined, or scheduling flexibility is desirable.

Expressing pipelining between output tiles:
- Cannot be expressed across separate launch instances: dependency tokens (the
  only token kind crossing the launch boundary) express ordering, not overlap.
- Correct patterns are within a single launch body:
  (a) Segment-level: two co-resident segments (compute + prefetch) linked by
      dependency tokens — co-residency guarantee ensures genuine overlap.
  (b) Loop-based: scf.for with iter_args-carried tokens for double-buffering
      (the pattern described in the scf.for section of §2.2).

Backend-specific concurrency behaviour table:
- GPU (ROCDL): launch iteration space → gpu.launch gridDim; always-parallel,
  all grid points dispatched concurrently subject to GPU occupancy.
- NPU (AIE): runtime-scheduled; instances may be sequential or concurrent
  depending on hardware partition availability. No concurrency guaranteed.
- Asymmetry is intentional: GPU is SIMT, NPU is task-queue-like.
- Recommend air.segment iteration spaces or concurrency tokens for programs
  requiring guaranteed minimum concurrency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: replace phantom herd reshape attribute with actual reshape controls

Gap 7: §2.3 said reshaping was 'prevented by a per-herd attribute that
disables reshaping' but no such attribute exists in the codebase.

Replace with what actually controls reshape behaviour on AIE:
- AIRCollapseHerdPass performs the reshape (collapse to 1D arrangement).
- Reshape is inhibited automatically when the herd uses cascade channels
  (channel_type = "cascade"), as cascade connections are topology-dependent.
- Placement attributes (x_loc, y_loc, x_size, y_size) on the enclosing
  segment constrain legal shapes by fixing the tile footprint.
- The pass accepts a max-col-size option to bound collapsed width.
- Note that a dedicated per-herd attribute to disable reshaping independently
  of these conditions is not yet implemented.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Python heredoc content at column 0 broke YAML block scalar parsing.
Extracted to scripts/apply-pb-rules.py and call via run: step using
external PocketBase URL (port 8091 is publicly accessible).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: POS-FARM
repository.description: Aplikasi penjualan

commit.message:
Switch from H1 to G1, fix critical state indexing bug, make timing tunable

- Replace Unitree H1 with G1 (29 position-controlled actuators, kp=500)
- Fix cost function: mjSTATE_FULLPHYSICS includes time at index 0,
  so qpos/qvel indexing was off by one (reading garbage before)
- Move SIM_DT, CONTROL_DT, CONTROL_SUBSTEPS to mpc.py so agent can tune them
- prepare.py imports timing from mpc lazily to avoid circular imports
- Baseline: 1.246 m/s (G1) vs -0.049 m/s (H1 with bug)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove setup-gcloud action from CI workflows (#14)

The setup-gcloud@v2 action now requires authentication, but we only
need gcloud to install the Firestore emulator. Since gcloud is
pre-installed on GitHub runners, we can call gcloud directly.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: google-adk-firestore-session
repository.description: Firestore session package extracted from wisal monorepo

commit.message:
change what-we-build card titles to var(--text) instead of amber

On the light grey background --text resolves to #0d0d0d (near-black),
giving proper contrast and matching user request.
